# unifi_acl_rule (Resource)

Manage a UniFi ACL rule. The active nested filter blocks depend on `type`.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Manage an ACL rule enforced by UniFi switching.
data "unifi_site" "main" {
  name = "Default"
}

resource "unifi_network" "iot" {
  site_id                 = data.unifi_site.main.id
  management              = "GATEWAY"
  name                    = "iot"
  enabled                 = true
  vlan_id                 = 30
  isolation_enabled       = true
  cellular_backup_enabled = false
  internet_access_enabled = true
  mdns_forwarding_enabled = false

  ipv4_configuration = {
    auto_scale_enabled = false
    host_ip_address    = "10.30.0.1"
    prefix_length      = 24
  }
}

resource "unifi_acl_rule" "block_iot_dns" {
  site_id         = data.unifi_site.main.id
  type            = "IPV4"
  enabled         = true
  name            = "block-iot-dns"
  action          = "BLOCK"
  protocol_filter = ["TCP", "UDP"]

  source_ip_filter = {
    type        = "NETWORKS"
    network_ids = [unifi_network.iot.id]
  }

  destination_ip_filter = {
    type  = "PORTS"
    ports = [53]
  }
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `action` (String) ACL rule action. Supported values: `ALLOW`, `BLOCK`.
- `enabled` (Boolean)
- `name` (String)
- `site_id` (String)
- `type` (String) ACL rule type. Supported values: `IPV4`, `MAC`.

### Optional

- `description` (String)
- `destination_ip_filter` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--destination_ip_filter))
- `destination_mac_filter` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--destination_mac_filter))
- `enforcing_device_ids` (Set of String) Optional list of switch-capable device IDs where the ACL rule is enforced. When omitted, the rule applies to all compatible switches.
- `network_id_filter` (String) Required for `MAC` ACL rules. The network ID to which the rule applies.
- `protocol_filter` (Set of String) Optional protocol filter for `IPV4` ACL rules. Supported values: `TCP`, `UDP`.
- `source_ip_filter` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--source_ip_filter))
- `source_mac_filter` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--source_mac_filter))

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) The ID of this resource.
- `index` (Number) Controller-assigned ACL rule ordering index.

<a id="nestedatt--destination_ip_filter"></a>
### Nested Schema for `destination_ip_filter`

Required:

- `type` (String)

Optional:

- `ip_addresses_or_subnets` (Set of String)
- `network_ids` (Set of String)
- `ports` (Set of Number)


<a id="nestedatt--destination_mac_filter"></a>
### Nested Schema for `destination_mac_filter`

Required:

- `mac_addresses` (Set of String)
- `type` (String)

Optional:

- `prefix_length` (Number)


<a id="nestedatt--source_ip_filter"></a>
### Nested Schema for `source_ip_filter`

Required:

- `type` (String)

Optional:

- `ip_addresses_or_subnets` (Set of String)
- `network_ids` (Set of String)
- `ports` (Set of Number)


<a id="nestedatt--source_mac_filter"></a>
### Nested Schema for `source_mac_filter`

Required:

- `mac_addresses` (Set of String)
- `type` (String)

Optional:

- `prefix_length` (Number)

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
# Import an ACL rule by site and rule identifier.
terraform import unifi_acl_rule.block_iot_dns <site_id>/<acl_rule_id>
```
